hypothesis: If SBI and SAA-guided diffusion trained only on metastable-state data sample transition-state configurations by interpolating conditional scores and applying force-aligned ascent, then the resulting transition-state configurations and reaction-coordinate estimates will be more accurate than those from linear or geodesic interpolation because the combination of score-based interpolation and TS-focused ascent explicitly steers sampling toward true transition-state regions without requiring explicit TS data.
